assumptions: The developer uses macOS, Linux, or Windows with a terminal and browser.
No Fly.io account, flyctl auth session, or Fly App exists yet.
The HelloFly demo route does not require local application source.

developer_goal: Create a Fly.io account with flyctl and launch a first app whose fly.dev URL opens in a browser.

first_success.outcome: A Fly App is deployed and reachable at its https://<app-name>.fly.dev URL.
first_success.observable_signal: After fly status and fly apps open, the browser shows the deployed app at the fly.dev hostname.

route_selection.rule: Official Getting started presents peer Fly Launch routes; lexicographic selection is unresolved between HelloFly demo and own-app Quickstart because billing timing and conditional deploy differ.

candidates.name: Launch HelloFly demo image
candidates.reason: Fully specified image path with explicit first-app success, but credit-card prompt timing remains unspecified.

candidates.name: Quickstart: Launch your own app
candidates.reason: Listed first on Getting started, but requires project source and may add a conditional fly deploy.

candidates.name: Framework-specific getting started
candidates.reason: Requires a language or framework choice before launch.

required_path.action: Install flyctl using the documented command for the local operating system.
required_path.observable_result: The fly command is available in the terminal.

required_path.action: Run fly auth signup and complete the browser signup flow.

required_fields.label: Email and password or OAuth consent details
required_fields.notes: Docs name identity providers but do not enumerate the live form controls.

required_path.observable_result: flyctl returns an authenticated CLI session.

required_path.action: If prompted before deployment, provide payment information required by the account state.

required_fields.label: Credit card payment information
required_fields.notes: Sign-up docs say a card prompt appears at some point; Free Trial docs describe launching without adding a card until limits are hit.

required_path.observable_result: The account is eligible to continue launching Machines under the applicable trial or billing state.

required_path.action: Run the selected launch command (HelloFly image or fly launch in project source) and accept or confirm settings.
required_path.observable_result: Fly creates the app, writes fly.toml when applicable, and starts deployment.

required_path.action: Run fly status, then fly apps open to open the deployed app.
required_path.observable_result: The browser opens the app's fly.dev URL.

prerequisites.description: A local machine that can install flyctl and open a browser for auth.
Own-app Quickstart additionally requires project source Fly Launch can scan or a Dockerfile.

external_gates.description: Browser signup with email, Google, or GitHub must complete for flyctl authentication.
Payment method may be required depending on trial exhaustion or account prompt timing.

unavoidable_waits.description: Fly must retrieve the image or build, provision IPs, and launch Machines before the URL serves traffic.

platform_outcomes.description: fly launch creates the app and fly.toml and deploys Machines for the demo or detected project.
A successful deploy exposes an https://<app>.fly.dev hostname.

excluded.reason: Docs are evidence for route selection, not product actions.

excluded.item: Optional settings tweak UI during launch
excluded.reason: Demo guide allows accepting defaults with n.

excluded.item: fly apps destroy and later fly deploy changes
excluded.reason: Cleanup and iteration occur after first success.

sources.title: fly auth signup
evidence_supported: Browser signup form opened by CLI

sources.title: Sign up and sign in to Fly.io

uncertainties.question: At exactly which point does a new account receive a credit-card prompt relative to first deploy?
uncertainties.impact: Billing may or may not be a required action before first success, changing action count and external gates.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fresh-account walkthrough dated against current Free Trial and signup flows showing whether launch succeeds without a card.

uncertainties.question: Is HelloFly demo or own-app Quickstart the lexicographic shortest required route under SELECTION-POLICY?
uncertainties.impact: Own-app adds source prerequisites and possible fly deploy; demo avoids source but is not listed first.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human judgment applying lexicographic rules to the two documented peer Getting started routes.
